problem_text: Use a comparison test to determine whether sum_{n=1}^infinity 1/(n^2 + 1) converges or diverges.

content.label: Positive-term series
content.applies_when: the terms are positive, so a comparison test applies

content.meaning: sum 1/n^p converges if and only if p > 1; here the comparison series 1/n^2 has p = 2 and converges.

content.label: choose the comparison series sum 1/n^2, a convergent p-series (p=2)
content.action: choose the comparison series sum 1/n^2, a convergent p-series (p=2)
content.purpose: pick a known convergent series to bound the given one

content.label: establish 0 < 1/(n^2 + 1) < 1/n^2 for all n >= 1
content.purpose: show the given terms are dominated by the convergent series

content.label: Direct Comparison Test (convergence)
content.applies_when: a nonnegative series bounded above term-by-term by a convergent series also converges.

content.label: since sum 1/n^2 converges and the given terms are smaller, sum 1/(n^2 + 1) converges by Direct Comparison
content.action: since sum 1/n^2 converges and the given terms are smaller, sum 1/(n^2 + 1) converges by Direct Comparison
